fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), which incorrectly allows a new read when the number of active reads is exactly equal to the limit. It should use a greater-than-or-equal-to comparison (`>=`) to deny access at the boundary.

constraints.intent: The buggy comparison should behave like a correct greater-than-or-equal comparison.
If access is allowed, the number of active reads must be strictly less than the limit.

spec.expr: all(ts >= cutoff__1 for ts in active__1)

constraints.intent: All timestamps considered active must be within the time window.
A read should be allowed if and only if the number of active reads is less than the limit.
